articles.title: ‘For Example, Putin’: How Xi Used a Private Garden Walk to Charm Trump
articles.summary: Watch an exchange between the two men in which the Chinese leader sought to signal exclusivity and name-dropped Russia’s president.

articles.title: Oil Prices Rise as Trump-Xi Summit Yields No Clear Breakthroughs on Iran War
articles.summary: Hopes for an end to the war in Iran faded after President Trump failed to secure a commitment from China to help persuade Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Trump Touts ‘Fantastic Trade Deals’ With China, but Details Are Scarce
articles.summary: The president left Beijing following a summit with China, during which the two countries sought to stabilize their economic and political relations.

articles.title: SCOTUS upholds abortion pill telehealth access. And, Trump returns from China visit
articles.summary: The Supreme Court kept access to the abortion pill mifepristone available via telehealth. And, Trump says he made multiple trade deals during his state visit to China.

articles.title: Key takeaways from Trump's China trip
articles.summary: President Trump returns to the U.S. after wrapping up his whirlwind trip to China.

articles.title: China economist shares takeaways from the Trump-Xi summit
articles.summary: Steve Inskeep speaks with Chinese economist Keyu Jin about what came out of the Trump-Xi summit.

articles.title: Morning news brief
articles.summary: Trump returns to U.S. after trip to China, Supreme Court decides to maintain abortion pill access, U.K. prime minister faces challenges from his own party.

articles.title: How the relationship between the U.S. and China has changed under Trump
articles.summary: NPR's Steve Inskeep talks with Chinese scholar Da Wei about the history of the U.S.-China relationship and how it has changed under President Trump.

articles.title: At this electronics market in China, buyers can find parts for just about everything
articles.summary: NPR's Steve Inskeep visits a massive electronics market in Shenzhen, China, where buyers can source parts for just about anything.

articles.title: In Photos and Video: President Trump Goes to China
articles.summary: Mr. Trump’s trip, the first U.S. presidential visit to China in almost a decade, was closely watched for signs of whether a trade truce between the countries would hold.

articles.title: A Surge of U.S. Spy Planes Over Cuba, and Retailers’ $20 Billion Bet on Physical Stores
articles.summary: Plus, the Friday news quiz.

articles.title: Death toll in attack on Kyiv apartment building now stands at 24
articles.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Friday that a Russian missile attack on a Kyiv apartment building the previous day killed 24 people, including three children.

articles.title: As Powell Steps Down, the Fed Confronts ‘Regime Change’
articles.summary: Jerome H. Powell is passing the chair’s baton to Kevin M. Warsh at the Federal Reserve, an institution that President Trump’s pick says needs an overhaul.

articles.title: It’s Not Just U.S. Stocks. A.I. and Oil Are Moving Global Markets, Too.
articles.summary: Intel, along with Taiwan and South Korea, are the latest winners, our columnist says. True diversification is hard to find.

articles.title: Which Trump cabinet member has a new reality show? The quiz knows
articles.summary: This week, in Warshington, D.C., the Senate confirmed Kevin Warsh as the next chair of the Federal Reserve and we wrote a quiz question about his name. Enjoy that, and the other nine, too.

articles.title: After redistricting, what does representation mean to Tennessee voters?
articles.summary: What does representation look like for Tennessee voters who were split into three new congressional districts last week? NPR traveled from Memphis into the Nashville suburbs to ask.

articles.title: Gen Z homeowners? Yes, more in their 20s are managing to buy despite the odds
articles.summary: Gen Z homeowners now outpace millennials at the same age. They're more likely to be single and less likely to use help from parents.

articles.title: U.S. pledges additional $1.8B in funding for U.N. humanitarian aid efforts
articles.summary: The Trump administration announced $1.8 billion in funding for the U.N.'s global humanitarian relief efforts.

articles.title: Inside Beijing's Temple of Heaven
articles.summary: NPR's Steve Inskeep and Jennifer Pak visit the Temple of Heaven in Beijing.

articles.title: Son reflects with his mother about growing up with autism
articles.summary: Jhovana Figueroa was diagnosed with autism when he was a toddler. For StoryCorps, Figueroa and his mom talk about his childhood and their hopes for the future.

articles.title: How Jerome Powell Managed a Chaotic Era
articles.summary: Our reporter Colby Smith, who covers the Federal Reserve and the economy, looks back at Jerome Powell’s tenure as chair of the Fed.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
